message:
fix(core): support multi-source-block decode in RaptorQ codec and WAL FEC adapter

The previous SymbolCodec::decode() implementation hardcoded
source_blocks=1, which produced incorrect ObjectParams when the encoded
data spanned multiple source blocks. The inline decode geometry
reconstruction is extracted into a standalone decode_object_params()
helper that computes source_blocks and symbols_per_block from k_source,
symbol_size, and max_block_size -- matching the partitioning the encoder
would have used.

In raptorq_integration.rs, a now-redundant try_into() conversion on
source_blocks (already u16) is removed.

FecCommitHook::set_enabled(false) now calls discard_buffered() to drop
any accumulated WAL frames, preventing stale data from being encoded
into the next FEC commit after the hook is re-enabled. A test confirms
that only frames received after re-enabling are included.

A new test_fec_encode_then_recover_multiple_source_blocks test in
wal_fec_adapter validates end-to-end encode/decode with max_block_size
set small enough to force multi-block partitioning, and a corresponding
test_codec_decode_multiple_source_blocks_roundtrip in raptorq_codec
verifies the codec layer in isolation.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1106,12 +1106,7 @@ mod tests {
                     what: "symbol_size as u16".to_owned(),
                     value: symbol_size.to_string(),
                 })?,
-                source_blocks
-                    .try_into()
-                    .map_err(|_| FrankenError::OutOfRange {
-                        what: "source_blocks".to_owned(),
-                        value: source_blocks.to_string(),
-                    })?,
+                source_blocks,
                 u16::try_from(symbols_per_block).map_err(|_| FrankenError::OutOfRange {
                     what: "symbols_per_block as u16".to_owned(),
                     value: symbols_per_block.to_string(),
